title: About | uOttawa Digital Policy Association
description: Learn about the uOttawa Digital Policy Association, a student-led, non-partisan organization exploring technology, law, governance, and public policy.
heading: uOttawa Digital Policy Association
subheading: Student-led dialogue on technology, law, governance, and public policy.
paragraph-1: uOttawa Digital Policy Association (uODPA) is a student-led, non-partisan organization at the University of Ottawa dedicated to exploring the intersection of technology, law, governance, and public policy. We examine how emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, privacy and data protection, cybersecurity, platform regulation, and intellectual property, are governed, regulated, and debated.
paragraph-2: Through panels, workshops, roundtables, competitions, and other events, uODPA creates opportunities for students to engage with the policy challenges shaping the digital age. We bring together students from across disciplines to connect with professionals, deepen their understanding of digital governance, and contribute to thoughtful and informed discussion on the future of technology and society.
paragraph-3: Open to students from all faculties, uODPA fosters an inclusive and interdisciplinary community grounded in curiosity, critical thinking, and innovation.
object-1: uODPA unites members under the common goal of exploring and advancing the intersection of technology, law, governance, and public policy.
object-2: We promote discussion and innovation in digital governance, technology regulation, cybersecurity policy, artificial intelligence policy, and the societal impacts of emerging technologies.
activities: uODPA organizes workshops, panel discussions, hackathons, webinars, networking events, roundtable discussions, and student-led conversations related to technology, law, governance, and digital policy.
